synthesis_evidence.code_complete_on_disk: git diff HEAD -- src/renderer/game/scenes/WorldMapScene.js shows ~308 lines changed (additions + modifications) including the full revealNode/_spawnNodeObjects/_playRevealAnimation method trio. node --check passes. npm run build passes (333 modules transformed in 11.67s).
synthesis_evidence.import_extension_correct: Line 21: extended discoverySystem import to add revealArea + getDiscoveryState (was: isDiscovered + DISCOVERY_TILE_SIZE only). All four are used by the new methods.
discovery_gating_applied: Base/biome layer loop — skip iteration if !isDiscovered(x, y) (line 200ish)
Node layer loop (circles/icons/labels) — skip iteration if !isDiscovered(x, y) (line 270ish)
Path layer — skip path segment if destination !isDiscovered(bx, by) (line 497ish). Home/neighborhood treated as always-visible source — confirmed by inline comment.
Home/neighborhood marker block (separate from WORLD_LOCATIONS loop) NOT modified — intentional, keeps player's start point visible.
synthesis_evidence.reveal_method_present: revealNode(nodeId) class method on WorldMapScene. 8-step orchestration: lookup loc, validate _mapLayout, compute coords, detect first-ever via getDiscoveryState().tiles.length===0, revealArea(x,y,80px), saveGame(state), redrawFog(), spawn-or-reuse node objects + animate, SFX with discover→reward_stinger fallback, first-discovery dialog ('Zuzu' speaker).
synthesis_evidence.spawned_node_helper: _spawnNodeObjects(loc, x, y, state) creates a Phaser Container at depth 1 holding circle/icon/label/stars at local origin. Click handlers wired identically to create()-time nodes. Container approach allows clean scale tween from 0 to 1.
synthesis_evidence.tween_helper: _playRevealAnimation({container, circle, icon, nameLabel}) — if container exists, scale 0→1 with Back.easeOut over 400 ms; alpha pulse on circle+icon for both paths (35% in, 65% out). Non-blocking; nameLabel alpha untouched to preserve locked-state 0.7 dimming.
synthesis_evidence.hmr_block_intact: registerSceneHmr(SCENE_KEY, import.meta.hot, WorldMapScene) at bottom of file — preserved verbatim per worker's own framework-summary 'HMR preserved at line 1550'.
synthesis_evidence.fog_layer_untouched: _renderFogLayer / redrawFog / _drawFogTiles methods are untouched in the diff. Worker is a redrawFog() consumer, not modifier — per dispatch instruction.

files_changed.diff_summary: +~280 net lines, -~10. Three new class methods (revealNode, _spawnNodeObjects, _playRevealAnimation), three discovery-gate early-returns in existing loops, one import-line extension, one _mapLayout assignment, one _nodeObjects map declaration.

design_decisions.skip_vs_silhouette: SKIP entirely (no silhouette). Reasoning per inline comment: 'Skipping entirely (not silhouette) prevents leaking world layout through the fog layer, consistent with the path-gating rule below.' Aligns with spec recommendation in dispatch prompt.
design_decisions.first_discovery_detection: Pre-revealArea check `getDiscoveryState().tiles.length === 0`. No save schema bump required. Edge case handled: if a player loads a save where the discovery set is non-empty, no first-discovery dialog fires (correct — they've discovered before).
design_decisions.reveal_radius: 80 px (2.5 × DISCOVERY_TILE_SIZE = 2.5 × 32). Covers node circle (r=22) + comfortable surrounding area aligned to fog grid.
design_decisions.discover_sfx: audioManifest has NO 'discover' SFX key. Worker confirmed by grep — manifest defines a 'desert_discovery' MUSIC track (mood:discovery, but currently 0-byte file per audio-asset-cleanup notes) and a 'reward_stinger' STINGER. Implementation tries hasSfx('discover') first (defensive against future addition), then falls back to playStinger('reward_stinger'). Recommend follow-up: a real `discover` SFX entry in audioManifest + a short audio file.

manual_verification_scenarios: 1. Fresh save (or reset) → world map shows: terrain visible, fog covering most tiles, only the home/neighborhood node visible (it's rendered separately and not in the discovery gate). No paths to other locations.

2. Trigger revealNode('arizona_desert') from devtools console (e.g., via window.__phaserGame.scene.getScene('WorldMapScene').revealNode('arizona_desert')) → arizona node appears with scale 0→1 Back.easeOut bounce + alpha pulse. reward_stinger SFX plays. First-time only: 'You discovered a new place! arizona_desert is now on your map.' dialog. Path from home to arizona becomes visible. Fog clears in a ~80 px radius around the node.

3. Reload page after revealing → arizona stays revealed (load path picks up persisted discovery state). No re-animation. Subsequent revealNode calls during the same session do NOT re-fire the first-discovery dialog (gating is per-save, not per-session).

follow_ups_recommended.home_fog_clearance: MEDIUM/LOW — Home/neighborhood node is always rendered (above fog), but the fog tile under the home is NOT pre-revealed on scene start. Player sees the home node clearly but the surrounding area is dark fog. Cosmetic. Fix in future polish: call revealArea(homeX, homeY, ~80) at scene create end-of-fog-rendering, OR seed initial discovery state in initDiscovery for the home tile.
follow_ups_recommended.spawned_vs_created_visual_asymmetry: LOW — Nodes rendered at create() time get the full visual treatment (drop shadow, biome anchor base, conditional difficulty stars, pulse animation if active quest). Nodes rendered via _spawnNodeObjects() get circle + icon + label + stars only — no biome anchor, no drop shadow. Visually they pop in cleanly but lack the geological anchor below. Mitigations: either replicate the biome-anchor logic in _spawnNodeObjects, or accept the asymmetry until next world-map-polish cycle.
follow_ups_recommended.discover_sfx_authoring: LOW — Truly correct SFX would be a short, gentle bell or chime. reward_stinger is loud and celebratory; works but slightly heavy for routine discoveries. Future audio cycle could compose a quieter discover.ogg.

scope_compliance.hmr_preserved: Top phaserHmr import (line 19) and bottom registerSceneHmr call preserved verbatim.
